posts.title: Posts
posts.subtitle: The school's established positions. Contracts are held against a post, and vacancies are reported for one.
posts.none: No posts have been set up yet
posts.deleteConfirm: Delete this post? This cannot be undone.
posts.subject: Subject

serviceTerms.title: Service Terms
serviceTerms.subtitle: The conditions of service a contract is held on — pay spine, increment timing, default hours and the pension schemes on offer.
serviceTerms.none: No service terms have been set up yet
serviceTerms.incrementDayHint: Defaults to the 1st of the month if left blank
serviceTerms.defaultsHint: Pre-filled onto a new contract, then overtypeable
serviceTerms.schemesHint: Tick the schemes available on this service term. Mark one as the main scheme to default new contracts to it.
serviceTerms.noSchemes: No pension schemes have been set up yet
serviceTerms.incrementRequired: An increment month is required when spinal progression is enabled.
serviceTerms.deleteConfirm: Delete this service term? This cannot be undone.
serviceTerms.spineOnPanel: The pay spine, its point range and its scales are set up on the service term page.

payScales.title: Pay Scales
payScales.subtitle: The pay spine — what each point is worth in each pay zone. Salaries are effective-dated, and a pay award appends the next version.
payScales.none: No pay scales have been set up yet
payScales.noRange: Set a range to generate points
payScales.noScales: No pay scales on this service term yet
payScales.spineSubtitle: The pay spine for this service term — its scales, their points, and what each point is worth.
payScales.spineHint: One spine for the whole service term. Each scale below is a window onto it, so an overlapping grade shares the same point and the same salary.
payScales.sharedSpineHint: Scales on a single pay spine share their points, so a salary typed against a point applies wherever that point appears.
payScales.rangeHint: Points are generated across this range at the service term's interval. Enter the salaries against them in the grid.
payScales.termRange: The service term allows points {{ range }}.
payScales.willGenerate: Generates {{ count }} points, {{ first }} to {{ last }}.
payScales.maxBelowMin: The maximum point cannot be below the minimum point.
payScales.rangeIncomplete: Enter both a minimum and a maximum point, or neither.
payScales.dropWarning: Saving removes {{ count }} point(s) and any salaries entered against them: {{ points }}
payScales.dropInUse: These points are used by existing contracts and cannot be removed: {{ points }}
payScales.deleteScaleConfirm: Remove {{ description }} and its points? Nothing is deleted until you save.
payScales.editHint: Changes apply to the version selected above. To record a new award, use New Pay Award instead so the previous version is kept.
payScales.awardHint: Uplift an existing version of the spine into a new one. The version being uplifted is kept and closed off the day before the new one starts.
payScales.perScaleHint: Leave blank to use the uplift above. Set a percentage to override it for that scale.
payScales.perScaleSpineHint: Each pay scale below runs its own range of points at its own interval. Use a single pay spine when grades share one set of points.
payScales.needEffectiveFrom: Choose an effective date before saving.
payScales.blockedByLockedPoints: Move staff off the highlighted points before removing them.
payScales.firstGenerationHint: No salaries yet. Pick the date these first take effect, fill in the grid, then save. Later changes go through New Pay Award so history is kept.
payScales.noSpineRange: Set the spine range above to generate points.
payScales.fixHighlighted: Fix the highlighted fields before saving.

err.maxBelowMin: Must be at or above the minimum
err.rangeIncomplete: Enter both a from and a to point
err.aboveSpine: Runs past the end of the spine
err.tooManyPoints: This range and interval make too many points

increment.title: Annual Increment
increment.hint: Move eligible staff up one spine point. Choose the date it takes effect, preview who moves and to what, then apply. Each move is recorded in salary history.
increment.noStaff: No staff on this service term are due an increment.
increment.summary: {{ eligible }} of {{ total }} can be incremented.
increment.cancelConfirm: Cancel the increment scheduled for {{ date }}?
increment.dueBody: A scheduled increment for this service term is due ({{ count }}). Review and run it.
increment.futureHint: In the future — use Schedule; it runs on the day.
